qr.about.label: About
qr.about.title: Free online QR code generator — full customisation, all content types, instant download
qr.about.body1: QR codes have become the universal bridge between physical and digital — on restaurant menus, product packaging, business cards, event posters, and museum exhibits worldwide. This free online QR code generator lets you create professional, fully customised QR codes for 8 different content types in seconds, with no account, no watermark, and no daily limit.
qr.about.body2: Choose from eight content types: URL (link to any website), vCard (digital business card that recipients can save directly to their phone contacts), plain text (display a message up to 300 characters), SMS (pre-fill a text message to a specific number), Email (pre-fill recipient, subject line, and body), WiFi (auto-connect guests to your network — no password typing required), Twitter/X (pre-compose a tweet), and Bitcoin (generate a cryptocurrency payment QR code with address and optional amount).
qr.about.body3: Every visual aspect of your QR code is customisable. Choose from five dot shapes — Square, Rounded, Dots, Classy, and Classy Rounded — to match your brand aesthetic. Select corner frame and eye styles to create a distinctive look. Set any foreground and background colour, or apply a multi-stop gradient for a modern finish. Upload your company logo or brand icon to embed it in the centre of the QR code. A real-time preview updates instantly as you adjust settings, so you always see exactly what you'll download before committing.
qr.about.body4: Download your finished QR code in four formats: PNG at resolutions up to 2000×2000 pixels for sharp digital use, SVG for infinitely scalable vector output ideal for large-format printing, JPG for maximum compatibility, or EPS for professional print workflows in Adobe Illustrator and InDesign. All generation happens locally in your browser — no data is sent to any server, keeping your WiFi passwords, business URLs, and contact details completely private.
qr.about.body5: QR codes generated by this tool follow the ISO/IEC 18004 standard and are compatible with all modern smartphone camera apps on iOS and Android — no third-party QR scanner app required. They work reliably at error correction level H (30% damage tolerance), meaning they still scan correctly even if partially obscured by a logo, decorative element, or minor print imperfection. Whether you are a marketer, restaurant owner, event organiser, developer, or small business — this tool delivers professional results without professional costs.

qr.privacy.body1: All QR code generation happens locally using JavaScript in your browser. Your WiFi passwords, business URLs, and contact details are never sent to any server.
qr.privacy.body2: Generate QR codes for sensitive information — WiFi credentials, private URLs, personal contact details — with complete confidence. No account required, no data stored.

qr.guide.s1.title: What is a QR Code
qr.guide.s1.p1: A QR code is a two-dimensional barcode that can store text, links, contact information, Wi-Fi credentials, and more. Users can scan it with their smartphone camera or a QR reader to quickly access the encoded information.
qr.guide.s1.p2: QR codes are widely used in restaurant menus, product packaging, business cards, event posters, museum exhibits, and more. They serve as a universal bridge connecting the physical and digital worlds.
qr.guide.s2.title: Static vs Dynamic QR Codes
qr.guide.s2.p1: Static QR codes encode content directly into the pattern and cannot be changed after generation. They never expire themselves, but if the linked URL becomes invalid, the QR code will no longer work.
qr.guide.s2.p2: Dynamic QR codes typically use a short link or backend redirect, allowing you to change the target URL later or track scan analytics. This tool generates static QR codes, giving you full control over the content.
qr.guide.s3.title: QR Code Types
qr.guide.s3.p1: ToolboxNova QR Code Generator supports 9 common types: URL links, plain text, Wi-Fi auto-connect, vCard business cards, email, phone, SMS, geographic location, and calendar events. Each type has a dedicated form to ensure standard format output.
qr.guide.s3.p2: URL QR codes are great for web promotion and marketing. Wi-Fi QR codes let visitors connect to your network instantly. vCard QR codes allow contact info to be saved with one tap. Calendar event QR codes are perfect for meetings and event scheduling.
qr.guide.s4.title: How to Create URL QR Codes
qr.guide.s4.p1: Select 'URL' from the type tabs and enter your web link. The system automatically adds the https:// prefix and validates the URL format. We recommend testing the QR code with your phone after generation to confirm the link works.
qr.guide.s4.p2: URL QR codes are the most common type, suitable for posters, business cards, product packaging, and store displays. Make sure your link remains accessible long-term to avoid QR code失效.
qr.guide.s5.title: How to Create Wi-Fi QR Codes
qr.guide.s5.p1: Select 'Wi-Fi', enter the network name (SSID), choose the encryption type (WPA/WPA2/WEP/None), and enter the password. When scanned, phones will automatically prompt to connect without manually typing the password.
qr.guide.s5.box: Wi-Fi QR codes contain the network name and password. Anyone who scans it can obtain the connection details. Only share with trusted people and avoid displaying QR codes with sensitive credentials in public.
qr.guide.s5.p2: For open networks without a password, select 'None'. For hidden networks, check the 'Hidden Network' option. We recommend testing with your own phone after generation to confirm it connects properly.
qr.guide.s6.title: How to Create vCard QR Codes
qr.guide.s6.p1: Select 'vCard' and fill in your name, phone, email, company, title, and address. The generated QR code follows the vCard standard. When scanned, phones will recognize it as a contact and allow saving directly to the address book.
qr.guide.s6.p2: vCard QR codes contain more data, resulting in a denser pattern. We recommend using a higher error correction level (Q or H) and ensuring sufficient print size (at least 3×3 cm) for reliable scanning.
qr.guide.s7.title: PNG vs SVG Format
qr.guide.s7.p1: PNG is a raster format ideal for websites, messaging, and everyday sharing. It offers the best compatibility. ToolboxNova supports PNG downloads up to 2000×2000 pixels, meeting most digital use cases.
qr.guide.s7.p2: SVG is a vector format that scales infinitely without losing quality. It's perfect for high-resolution printing, large posters, and post-editing needs. If your QR code will be printed on large materials, we strongly recommend downloading the SVG format.
qr.guide.s8.title: Colors and Error Correction
qr.guide.s8.p1: QR codes support custom foreground and background colors, but ensure sufficient contrast between them. Low contrast (e.g., light gray on white) can cause scan failures. We recommend classic dark-on-light combinations.
qr.guide.s8.box: Error correction level L recovers ~7% damage, M ~15%, Q ~25%, and H ~30%. If your QR code includes a logo or will be placed on rough surfaces, we recommend using Q or H level.
qr.guide.s8.p2: Higher error correction levels create more complex patterns but tolerate more遮挡. When adding a logo, the logo area should not exceed 15% of the total QR code area, and H level error correction is recommended.
qr.guide.s9.title: Adding Logos to QR Codes
qr.guide.s9.p1: Adding a logo makes your QR code more brand-recognizable. ToolboxNova supports uploading a company logo or brand icon in the center of the QR code, with adjustable size and margin.
qr.guide.s9.p2: Keep the logo small—no more than 15% of the total QR code area—and pair it with a higher error correction level (Q or H). Always test scanning with your phone before downloading to ensure the logo doesn't block critical recognition areas.
qr.guide.s10.title: QR Code Print Size Recommendations
qr.guide.s10.p1: Print size depends on the use case. For business cards, at least 2×2 cm; for posters and displays, at least 5×5 cm; for large outdoor ads, at least 15×15 cm.
qr.guide.s10.p2: The longer the encoded content, the larger the print size needed. If your QR code content exceeds 200 characters, we recommend a print size of at least 4×4 cm and using high-resolution PNG or SVG format.
qr.guide.s11.title: Why Won't My QR Code Scan
qr.guide.s11.p1: Common reasons include: insufficient color contrast, too small size, overly dense pattern from long content, logo too large blocking critical areas, insufficient margin, blurry printing, image compression artifacts, or physical damage to the code.
qr.guide.s11.p2: Solutions: ensure obvious contrast between foreground and background; maintain adequate margin (at least 4 module widths); use high-quality files for printing; test scanning with your phone before finalizing.
qr.guide.s12.title: Frequently Asked Questions
qr.guide.s12.p1: Q: Do QR codes expire? A: Static QR codes themselves don't expire, but if the linked URL becomes invalid, scanning will fail. Q: Is my input saved? A: ToolboxNova generates QR codes locally in your browser. Your input is never uploaded or stored.
qr.guide.s12.p2: Q: Can I add multiple links? A: Standard QR codes can only store one target. For multiple links, use a URL QR code pointing to a landing page. Q: Will all phones scan it? A: Our QR codes follow the ISO/IEC 18004 standard and are compatible with modern iOS and Android scanning features.
